title: Insights Analyst
skills: Interpret customer requirements, translate them into clear reporting deliverables, including generating dashboards, query building and recurring or ad hoc report formats.

Conduct quantitative and qualitative analysis of earned and owned media data, including social listening outputs, news coverage, and content performance, to identify key narratives, opportunities, and risks.
Craft contextual insights based on performance metrics and KPIs, turning raw data into clear, concise storylines.
Maintain visibility across reporting timelines, proactively communicate progress or blockers, and ensure methodological consistency and quality across all deliverables
Uphold visual and formatting consistency across deliverables, proofreading for clarity, data integrity, and narrative cohesion
Address customer inquiries and concerns regarding delivered reports, contributing to training and initiatives to enhance team knowledge and efficiency.
Conduct quantitative and qualitative analysis of data to provide clients with insights on brand perception, performance, and industry trends.
Own the reporting workflow from setup to delivery, ensuring clarity in expectations, smooth coordination with internal teams, and high-quality outputs for clients
Utilize Meltwater software, Excel, and PowerPoint to extract data, synthesize findings, and package insights in a way that informs strategic decisions.
Develop strategies to improve report quality and objectives based on client feedback, ensuring timely completion of deliverables with high quality by effectively coordinating with the team.
Bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as Social Sciences, Communications, Marketing, Media Studies, Data Analytics, or related fields emphasizing research and analysis.
0-2 years of relevant work experience (client-facing roles are advantageous).
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills in English, with a particular emphasis on proficient report writing in English.
Proficiency in Microsoft PowerPoint & Excel, including PivotTable, VLOOKUP, and similar functions.
Meticulous attention to detail, adept at handling recurring tasks and ensuring data integrity.
Proficient report writing skills, covering both qualitative and quantitative aspects.
Customer-focused mindset with a commitment to exceptional client servicing.
Critical thinking skills, facilitating insights derived from data analysis.
Strong interest in current affairs, mainstream media, and social media trends.
Comfortable working autonomously and collaborating effectively with remote team members, including reporting to managers situated in distant locations.
A keen interest in learning, adaptability, and resilience in a dynamic work environment.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, particularly in report writing.
Willingness to embrace the best of both worlds with our hybrid work schedule. This role requires you to be in the office 3 days a week.
The ability to legally work in the country of hire is required for this position.
Enjoy comprehensive paid time off that allows you to have an enhanced work-life balance.
Excellent medical, dental, and vision options
Complimentary CalmApp subscription for you and your loved ones, because mental wellness matters.
Energetic work environment with a hybrid work style, providing the balance you need.
Thrive within our inclusive community and seize ongoing professional development opportunities to elevate your career
description:
About the job

Description

What We’re Looking For

Join Meltwater's Client Insights team as Insights Analyst, where you'll play a key role in transforming data into meaningful narratives for our clients. You’ll contribute to bespoke reporting, using both quantitative and qualitative analysis to uncover trends, identify opportunities, and guide strategic decisions.

In this role, you’ll take ownership of report delivery workflows, coordinating internally as needed to ensure insights are delivered with consistency, clarity, and impact. Your work will blend data storytelling, critical thinking, and collaborative execution. At Meltwater, you'll immerse yourself in an environment fostering continuous growth, where mentorship and inclusive leadership are fundamental principles rather than mere catchphrases. Collaborate with experienced colleagues and respected leaders, continually evolving alongside industry trends. Within this dynamic environment, you'll thrive within a culture emphasizing empowerment and autonomy, making significant contributions to our expanding organization's success.

What You'll Do:


Interpret customer requirements, translate them into clear reporting deliverables, including generating dashboards, query building and recurring or ad hoc report formats. 
Conduct quantitative and qualitative analysis of earned and owned media data, including social listening outputs, news coverage, and content performance, to identify key narratives, opportunities, and risks. 
Craft contextual insights based on performance metrics and KPIs, turning raw data into clear, concise storylines. 
Maintain visibility across reporting timelines, proactively communicate progress or blockers, and ensure methodological consistency and quality across all deliverables
Uphold visual and formatting consistency across deliverables, proofreading for clarity, data integrity, and narrative cohesion
Address customer inquiries and concerns regarding delivered reports, contributing to training and initiatives to enhance team knowledge and efficiency. 
Conduct quantitative and qualitative analysis of data to provide clients with insights on brand perception, performance, and industry trends. 
Own the reporting workflow from setup to delivery, ensuring clarity in expectations, smooth coordination with internal teams, and high-quality outputs for clients
Utilize Meltwater software, Excel, and PowerPoint to extract data, synthesize findings, and package insights in a way that informs strategic decisions. 
Develop strategies to improve report quality and objectives based on client feedback, ensuring timely completion of deliverables with high quality by effectively coordinating with the team. 


What You'll Bring:


Bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as Social Sciences, Communications, Marketing, Media Studies, Data Analytics, or related fields emphasizing research and analysis. 
0-2 years of relevant work experience (client-facing roles are advantageous). 
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills in English, with a particular emphasis on proficient report writing in English. 
Proficiency in Microsoft PowerPoint & Excel, including PivotTable, VLOOKUP, and similar functions. 
Meticulous attention to detail, adept at handling recurring tasks and ensuring data integrity. 
Proficient report writing skills, covering both qualitative and quantitative aspects. 
Customer-focused mindset with a commitment to exceptional client servicing. 
Critical thinking skills, facilitating insights derived from data analysis. 
Strong interest in current affairs, mainstream media, and social media trends. 
Comfortable working autonomously and collaborating effectively with remote team members, including reporting to managers situated in distant locations. 
A keen interest in learning, adaptability, and resilience in a dynamic work environment. 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, particularly in report writing. 
Willingness to embrace the best of both worlds with our hybrid work schedule. This role requires you to be in the office 3 days a week. 
The ability to legally work in the country of hire is required for this position. 


What We Offer:


Enjoy comprehensive paid time off that allows you to have an enhanced work-life balance. 
Excellent medical, dental, and vision options
Complimentary CalmApp subscription for you and your loved ones, because mental wellness matters. 
Energetic work environment with a hybrid work style, providing the balance you need. 
Thrive within our inclusive community and seize ongoing professional development opportunities to elevate your career


Where You’ll Work: Lago Alberto 375, Suite 200 Granada, Miguel Hidalgo 11320 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico

Our Story

At Meltwater, we believe that when you have the right people in the right environment, great things happen.

Our best-in-class technology empowers our 27,000 customers around the world to make better business decisions through data. But we can’t do that without our global team of developers, innovators, problem-solvers, and high-performers who embrace challenges and find new solutions for our customers.

Our award-winning global culture drives everything we do and creates an environment where our employees can make an impact, learn every day, feel a sense of belonging, and celebrate each other’s successes along the way.

We are innovators at the core who see the potential in people, ideas and technologies. Together, we challenge ourselves to go big, be bold, and build best-in-class solutions for our customers.

We’re proud of our diverse team of 2,200+ employees in 50 locations across 25 countries around the world. No matter where you are, you’ll work with people who care about your success and get the support you need to unlock new heights in your career.

We are Meltwater. Inspired by innovation, powered by people.

Equal Employment Opportunity Statement

Meltwater is an Equal Opportunity Employer and Prohibits Discrimination and Harassment of Any Kind: At Meltwater, we are dedicated to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace where every employee feels valued, respected, and empowered. We are committed to the principle of equal employment opportunity and strive to provide a work environment that is free from discrimination and harassment.

All employment decisions at Meltwater are made based on business needs, job requirements, and individual qualifications, without regard to race, color, religion or belief, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, marital status, veteran status, or any other status protected by the applicable laws and regulations.

Meltwater does not tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind, and we actively promote a culture of respect, fairness, and inclusivity. We encourage applicants of all backgrounds, experiences, and abilities to apply and join us in our mission to drive innovation and make a positive impact in the world.

We may use AI to assist in aspects of our hiring process, such as reviewing candidate information, capturing interview notes, and supporting interview activities. These tools are used only to assist our team through increased efficiencies. Every output continues to be reviewed by humans, and all hiring decisions are made by humans. We remain committed to evaluating candidates fairly, consistently, and in compliance with all applicable laws

title: Market Researcher
skills: Support IDC’s market research agenda by conducting in-depth primary and secondary research into IT markets and their impact on industries.
Track, monitor, and identify IT industry developments to identify and report on market shifts, patterns, and anomalies.
Collaborate with internal subject matter expert analysts to achieve research goals and produce deliverables efficiently.
Participate in ongoing professional development and contribute to team knowledge sharing.
Synthesize raw data into information and meaningful insights and deliver tailored content components and assets that address key business requirements.
Contribute to the creation of written research studies and presentations summarizing findings for internal and external stakeholders.
Bachelor’s degree required; (business, economics, management information systems, technology marketing, product management or related field).
2-3 years of experience in market research, consulting, or analysis, ideally in the information technology sector,
Previous experience in roles such as market intelligence or competitive intelligence is preferred.
Outstanding written and strong verbal communication skills.
Proven ability to manage multiple assignments and meet tight deadlines.
Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to collaborate with and across teams globally.
Experience with office productivity tools such as Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
Some experience with any AI tools such as MS CoPilot, Google Gemini, Perplexity, ChatGPT, or other similar tools.

A strong interest in the IT market, including, but not limited in topics such as AI and data management, security, enterprise applications, enterprise infrastructure, devices, networking, telecommunications, application development, and services.
Understanding and interest in the application of information technology to industry use cases.
Flexibility to work both independently as well as collaboratively in a fast-paced, global environment.
Professional fluency in English.
Experience working with or for technology vendors, IT distributors, or in a market research/consulting firm.
Experience working in or analyzing emerging markets within the technology sector.
Knowledge of business models, go-to-market strategies, and technology adoption patterns in enterprise IT.
Intellectual curiosity, willingness to learn new marker research methods and tools, analytical mindset, and a collaborative, supportive approach to teamwork.
description:
About the job

Overview

About the Role & Team

IDC, a global leader in technology market intelligence, is seeking a market researcher to join our team . This role requires a mix of analytical abilities, attention to detail, proficiency in research methodologies, alongside excellent communication skills to convey complex information effectively. The market researcher will play a crucial role in interpreting data, staying abreast of IT market trends and events , supporting their team with in-depth research, and work ing collaboratively to meet research objectives. The market researcher will play a crucial role in supporting the authorship and delivery of IDC’s market intelligence products and services in a rapidly evolving information technology landscape. The role will focus on utilizing the latest market research methodologies and tools to uncover relevant data from internal and external data sources and synthesizing into market research content assets.

What You’ll Do


Support IDC’s market research agenda by conducting in-depth primary and secondary research into IT markets and their impact on industries.
Track, monitor, and identify IT industry developments to identify and report on market shifts, patterns, and anomalies.
Collaborate with internal subject matter expert analysts to achieve research goals and produce deliverables efficiently.
Participate in ongoing professional development and contribute to team knowledge sharing.
Synthesize raw data into information and meaningful insights and deliver tailored content components and assets that address key business requirements.
Contribute to the creation of written research studies and presentations summarizing findings for internal and external stakeholders.


What You Bring


Bachelor’s degree required; (business, economics, management information systems, technology marketing, product management or related field).
2-3 years of experience in market research, consulting, or analysis, ideally in the information technology sector,
Previous experience in roles such as market intelligence or competitive intelligence is preferred.
Outstanding written and strong verbal communication skills.
Proven ability to manage multiple assignments and meet tight deadlines.
Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to collaborate with and across teams globally.
Experience with office productivity tools such as Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
Some experience with any AI tools such as MS CoPilot, Google Gemini, Perplexity, ChatGPT, or other similar tools.
A strong interest in the IT market, including, but not limited in topics such as AI and data management, security, enterprise applications, enterprise infrastructure, devices, networking, telecommunications, application development, and services.
Understanding and interest in the application of information technology to industry use cases.
Flexibility to work both independently as well as collaboratively in a fast-paced, global environment.
Professional fluency in English.


Preferred Attributes


Experience working with or for technology vendors, IT distributors, or in a market research/consulting firm.
Experience working in or analyzing emerging markets within the technology sector.
Knowledge of business models, go-to-market strategies, and technology adoption patterns in enterprise IT.
Intellectual curiosity, willingness to learn new marker research methods and tools, analytical mindset, and a collaborative, supportive approach to teamwork.


Why This Role Stands Out

At IDC, your work helps shape how the world understands technology and where it goes next. You collaborate with curious, high-caliber colleagues who value rigor, integrity, and shared success. As the premier global provider of trusted technology intelligence, IDC equips business and technology leaders with the evidence they need to make confident decisions. Our insights inform strategy, investment, and innovation across industries and regions.

Recognized by IIAR as Analyst Firm of the Year for five consecutive years, IDC sets the standard for credibility and impact. With more than 1,000 analysts worldwide and a truly global perspective, we combine deep expertise with practical relevance. Here, your ideas matter, your voice is heard, and your contributions provide the insights leaders rely on every day. It is meaningful work, backed by a culture that supports growth, collaboration, and long-term career development with a globally respected brand.

Equal Opportunity Employer

IDC is committed to providing equal employment opportunities for all qualified persons. Employment eligibility verification required. We participate in E-Verify.

title: Promotions Coordinator
skills: Provide tactical and operational support to the Sr Promotions Manager (Mexico/LATAM).

Handle the communication between the area and the creative agency, to brief, revise and approve creative concepts to be presented to potential third partners. Looks for new partner opportunities from different categories, verticals, trends and potential partnerships. Supports Sr. Promotions Manager in campaign presentations, promotional big ideas and ICO's (initial campaign overview).
Support communication between partners and the different NBCU business contacts.
Local/regional monitoring of the product exchange operational process. (Negotiation, follow up with contacts, ordering, shipping and reporting).
Local/regional monitoring of the premieres of each promotional partner in every LATAM country when we have regional partnerships. (Initial request, follow up with contacts and reporting).
Supports the Sr. Promotions Manager in pitching process.
Monitor and measure success of the local/regional promotional campaigns.
Creates campaign wrap reports (collects campaign testimonies, master graphics and key data from each partner/territory) that will serve as inspiration and support for future pitches.
Supports the Sr. Promotions Manager in servicing NBCU internal teams and divisions in Symphony projects (Networks, CP, TVD, Streaming, Parks, etc.) to send assets, AV materials and prize packages.
Coordinates promotional opportunities with local/regional exhibitors: Prize packages, menus, concessions, special formats, loyalty programs.
Promotional process with record labels and editorial publishers
Fills operation formats (PAF, timeline), pitch lists, NDA's.
Creates planning documents for internal purposes and marketing approvals (Local strategy/timeline for Mediacom)
Budget control administration (ODC, Budget adjustments)
Prize packages control and follow up with winners/ partner/agency.
Supports Promotions Manager in logistics during HO visits.
End to end premiums logistics and distribution.
Custom Characters Tour Calendar partners).
Collects information for promotional newsletters for tent pole films.
Schedule meetings, conference calls and arrangements.
Requires a high level of service to business partners (brands, media and exhibitors).
Highly proactive and organized person
Person with cordial, clear and concise communication. Strong verbal and written communication skills (working internationally)
Sense of Urgence
PR
Ability to work in a cross functional team
Teamwork and focus on detail
Fluent in English and Spanish
Graduated from Marketing, Communication, Administration, International Business careers.
2 or 3 years of work experience, success in supporting business, customer, product and/ or marketing plans.
Previous experience in entertainment is a plus
description:
About the job

NBCUniversal is one of the world's leading media and entertainment companies. We create world-class content, which we distribute across our portfolio of film, television, and streaming, and bring to life through our global theme park destinations, consumer products, and experiences. We own and operate leading entertainment and news brands, including NBC, NBC News, NBC Sports, Telemundo, NBC Local Stations, Bravo, and Peacock, our premium ad-supported streaming service. We produce and distribute premier filmed entertainment and programming through our powerhouse film and television studios, including Universal Pictures, DreamWorks Animation, and Focus Features, and the four global television studios under the Universal Studio Group banner, and operate industry-leading theme parks and experiences around the world through Universal Destinations & Experiences, including Universal Orlando Resort, home to Universal Epic Universe, and Universal Studios Hollywood. NBCUniversal is a subsidiary of Comcast Corporation. Visit www.nbcuniversal.com for more information.



Our impact is rooted in improving the communities where our employees, customers, and audiences live and work. We have a rich tradition of giving back and ensuring our employees have the opportunity to serve their communities. We champion an inclusive culture and strive to attract and develop a talented workforce to create and deliver a wide range of content reflecting our world.





Job Description



ROLE PURPOSE: In charge of strategic and operative promotional processes locally and regionally as well as the tactical implementation of Marketing activities related with third partners, media and exhibitions.

Support communication between partners and the different NBCU business contacts.

Supports the Sr. Promotions Manager in pitching process. 
Monitor and measure success of the local/regional promotional campaigns. 
Creates campaign wrap reports (collects campaign testimonies, master graphics and key data from each partner/territory) that will serve as inspiration and support for future pitches. 
Supports the Sr. Promotions Manager in servicing NBCU internal teams and divisions in Symphony projects (Networks, CP, TVD, Streaming, Parks, etc.) to send assets, AV materials and prize packages. 
Coordinates promotional opportunities with local/regional exhibitors: Prize packages, menus, concessions, special formats, loyalty programs. 
Promotional process with record labels and editorial publishers

Administrative Process.



Fills operation formats (PAF, timeline), pitch lists, NDA's.

Budget control administration (ODC, Budget adjustments)
Prize packages control and follow up with winners/ partner/agency. 
Supports Promotions Manager in logistics during HO visits. 
End to end premiums logistics and distribution. 
Custom Characters Tour Calendar partners).

Schedule meetings, conference calls and arrangements. 


Qualifications



Required skills & experience 



 



Requires a high level of service to business partners (brands, media and exhibitors).

description:
About the job

NBCUniversal is one of the world's leading media and entertainment companies. We create world-class content, which we distribute across our portfolio of film, television, and streaming, and bring to life through our global theme park destinations, consumer products, and experiences. We own and operate leading entertainment and news brands, including NBC, NBC News, NBC Sports, Telemundo, NBC Local Stations, Bravo, and Peacock, our premium ad-supported streaming service. We produce and distribute premier filmed entertainment and programming through our powerhouse film and television studios, including Universal Pictures, DreamWorks Animation, and Focus Features, and the four global television studios under the Universal Studio Group banner, and operate industry-leading theme parks and experiences around the world through Universal Destinations & Experiences, including Universal Orlando Resort, home to Universal Epic Universe, and Universal Studios Hollywood. NBCUniversal is a subsidiary of Comcast Corporation. Visit www.nbcuniversal.com for more information.



Our impact is rooted in improving the communities where our employees, customers, and audiences live and work. We have a rich tradition of giving back and ensuring our employees have the opportunity to serve their communities. We champion an inclusive culture and strive to attract and develop a talented workforce to create and deliver a wide range of content reflecting our world.





Job Description



ROLE PURPOSE: In charge of strategic and operative promotional processes locally and regionally as well as the tactical implementation of Marketing activities related with third partners, media and exhibitions.

Marketing tactics.

title: Meeting Coordinator
skills: Applies business program management system to lead programs.
Builds and handles reporting timeline, documents, and spreadsheets for assigned programs, as required by team and client.
Maintains accurate records, data input, and complete file management as outlined in Amex GBT Meetings & Events and client policies and procedures.
Crafts and delivers custom forms/reports to client and team such as sign-in sheets, breakout schedules, rooming lists, transportation, etc.
Process supplier invoices as required.
Reconciles events and expenses as needed
Bilingual English - Spanish
1 year of business experience in meetings industry
Solid understanding in pharma event industry preferred
6 months to 1 year of past meeting planning skills preferred
Experience in administrative tasks within the events field
Detail oriented
Microsoft Word and Excel experience – Intermediate/sophisticated level is a plus
Experience with meeting industry technology a plus, especially for attendee registration (CVENT)

Flexible benefits are tailored to each country and start the day you do. These include health and welfare insurance plans, retirement programs, parental leave, adoption assistance, and wellbeing resources to support you and your immediate family.
Travel perks: get a choice of deals each week from major travel providers on everything from flights to hotels to cruises and car rentals.

Develop the skills you want when the time is right for you, with access to over 20,000 courses on our learning platform, leadership courses, and new job openings available to internal candidates first.

We strive to champion Inclusion in every aspect of our business at Amex GBT. You can connect with colleagues through our global INclusion Groups, centered around common identities or initiatives, to discuss challenges, obstacles, achievements, and drive company awareness and action.
And much more!
description:
About the job

Amex GBT is a place where colleagues find inspiration in travel as a force for good and – through their work – can make an impact on our industry. We’re here to help our colleagues achieve success and offer an inclusive and collaborative culture where your voice is valued.

What You’ll Do On a Typical Day


Applies business program management system to lead programs.
Builds and handles reporting timeline, documents, and spreadsheets for assigned programs, as required by team and client.
Maintains accurate records, data input, and complete file management as outlined in Amex GBT Meetings & Events and client policies and procedures.
Crafts and delivers custom forms/reports to client and team such as sign-in sheets, breakout schedules, rooming lists, transportation, etc.
Process supplier invoices as required.
Reconciles events and expenses as needed



What We’re Looking For


Bilingual English - Spanish
1 year of business experience in meetings industry
Solid understanding in pharma event industry preferred 
6 months to 1 year of past meeting planning skills preferred
Experience in administrative tasks within the events field
Detail oriented
Microsoft Word and Excel experience – Intermediate/sophisticated level is a plus
Experience with meeting industry technology a plus, especially for attendee registration (CVENT)



Location

Mexico

Click here to learn more about the benefits we offer in Mexico.

The #TeamGBT Experience

Work and life: Find your happy medium at Amex GBT.


Flexible benefits are tailored to each country and start the day you do. These include health and welfare insurance plans, retirement programs, parental leave, adoption assistance, and wellbeing resources to support you and your immediate family.
Travel perks: get a choice of deals each week from major travel providers on everything from flights to hotels to cruises and car rentals.
Develop the skills you want when the time is right for you, with access to over 20,000 courses on our learning platform, leadership courses, and new job openings available to internal candidates first.
We strive to champion Inclusion in every aspect of our business at Amex GBT. You can connect with colleagues through our global INclusion Groups, centered around common identities or initiatives, to discuss challenges, obstacles, achievements, and drive company awareness and action.
And much more!



All applicants will receive equal consideration for employment without regard to age, sex, gender (and characteristics related to sex and gender), pregnancy (and related medical conditions), race, color, citizenship, religion, disability, or any other class or characteristic protected by law.

Click Here for Additional Disclosures in Accordance with the LA County Fair Chance Ordinance.

Furthermore, we are committed to providing reasonable accommodation to qualified individuals with disabilities. Please let your recruiter know if you need an accommodation at any point during the hiring process. For details regarding how we protect your data, please consult the Amex GBT Recruitment Privacy Statement.

What if I don’t meet every requirement? If you’re passionate about our mission and believe you’d be a phenomenal addition to our team, don’t worry about “checking every box;" please apply anyway. You may be exactly the person we’re looking for!

title: Content & SEO Specialist
skills: Eligibility: This position is open to candidates residing in Latin America.
Application Language: Please submit your CV in English. Applications submitted in other languages will not be considered.

Professional Presentation: We encourage you to showcase your professional experience by including a Loom video in the application form. While this is optional, candidates who provide a video presentation will be given priority.
Interview Policy: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) tools during interviews are strictly prohibited.

Note: As part of the final stages of our hiring process, candidates may be asked to provide professional references for a reference check. If hired, you may also be asked to update your LinkedIn profile :)
AI-Assisted Blog Content → CMS Publishing
Use AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, or similar) to generate first drafts from content briefs and keywords
Rewrite and refine AI output so it reads natural, human, and brand-consistent
Fact-check, improve clarity, and ensure editorial quality (no “AI-sounding” content)
Publish 2–4 blog posts per week into a CMS (proper formatting: H1/H2/H3, meta title, meta description, slug)
Source and integrate relevant visuals for every post (hero images, diagrams, screenshots where relevant)
Ensure images are contextually relevant, correctly sized, and include alt text
Add internal links following the hub-and-spoke structure
Implement FAQ schema markup where applicable (training provided)
Maintain tracking in the content calendar (status, publish date, URL, performance notes)
Weekly Newsletter
Draft one newsletter per week in an email platform (e.g., Klaviyo or equivalent)
Curate 2–3 external insights (industry news, posts, tools, studies)
Write concise commentary and framing around each item
Include links to newly published content
Maintain a clear, non-promotional tone focused on value and relevance
Schedule, test, and send each issue
Monitor open and click-through rates weekly and flag performance trends
LinkedIn Content (1–2 Posts Weekly)
Write 1–2 posts per week for a company LinkedIn page
Avoid generic “corporate thought leadership” tone — keep it specific, practical, and grounded
Pull insights from blog content, product use cases, customer conversations, or sales insights
Create or source supporting visuals (screenshots, simple graphics, text-based visuals)
Schedule posts using a social scheduling tool or native platform
Monitor engagement and flag important comments or mentions for response
Template Pages & Gated Content
Write landing page copy for template downloads (based on provided briefs)
Format templates into usable assets (PDF, Slides, or Docs)
Publish gated landing pages in CMS with email capture integration
Ensure proper metadata, internal linking, and conversion flow
Maintain consistency between page copy and downloadable asset
Strong experience using AI writing tools effectively (knows the difference between edited output and raw AI content)
Ability to write clearly, directly, and without corporate or filler language
Experience publishing content in a CMS environment
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage workload independently
Ability to source relevant, context-aware visuals
Comfortable receiving editorial feedback and iterating quickly
Experience with CMS platforms (e.g., Webflow or similar)
Email marketing experience
Basic SEO understanding (metadata, structure, internal linking)
Experience writing for B2B audiences (sales, revenue, or SaaS context)
Familiarity with CRM ecosystems
Experience with LinkedIn content creation and distribution
description:
About the job

Hi there! We are South and our client is looking for a Content & SEO Specialist!

Note To Applicants


Eligibility: This position is open to candidates residing in Latin America. 
Application Language: Please submit your CV in English. Applications submitted in other languages will not be considered. 
Professional Presentation: We encourage you to showcase your professional experience by including a Loom video in the application form. While this is optional, candidates who provide a video presentation will be given priority. 
Interview Policy: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) tools during interviews are strictly prohibited. 
Note: As part of the final stages of our hiring process, candidates may be asked to provide professional references for a reference check. If hired, you may also be asked to update your LinkedIn profile :)


The Core Job (Non-Negotiable)

These Four Areas Define Success In This Role


 AI-Assisted Blog Content → CMS Publishing


You will execute a structured content strategy that includes keyword research, content briefs, and a hub-and-spoke architecture already in place.

Your responsibility is to turn briefs into high-quality, published content.


Use AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, or similar) to generate first drafts from content briefs and keywords
Rewrite and refine AI output so it reads natural, human, and brand-consistent
Fact-check, improve clarity, and ensure editorial quality (no “AI-sounding” content)
Publish 2–4 blog posts per week into a CMS (proper formatting: H1/H2/H3, meta title, meta description, slug)
Source and integrate relevant visuals for every post (hero images, diagrams, screenshots where relevant)
Ensure images are contextually relevant, correctly sized, and include alt text
Add internal links following the hub-and-spoke structure
Implement FAQ schema markup where applicable (training provided)
Maintain tracking in the content calendar (status, publish date, URL, performance notes)
 Weekly Newsletter


Own a weekly email newsletter sent to a B2B audience (sales and revenue professionals).


Draft one newsletter per week in an email platform (e.g., Klaviyo or equivalent)
Curate 2–3 external insights (industry news, posts, tools, studies)
Write concise commentary and framing around each item
Include links to newly published content
Maintain a clear, non-promotional tone focused on value and relevance
Schedule, test, and send each issue
Monitor open and click-through rates weekly and flag performance trends
 LinkedIn Content (1–2 Posts Weekly)


Manage consistent organic presence on LinkedIn.


Write 1–2 posts per week for a company LinkedIn page
Avoid generic “corporate thought leadership” tone — keep it specific, practical, and grounded
Pull insights from blog content, product use cases, customer conversations, or sales insights
Create or source supporting visuals (screenshots, simple graphics, text-based visuals)
Schedule posts using a social scheduling tool or native platform
Monitor engagement and flag important comments or mentions for response
 Template Pages & Gated Content


Build and publish downloadable, conversion-focused content assets.


Write landing page copy for template downloads (based on provided briefs)
Format templates into usable assets (PDF, Slides, or Docs)
Publish gated landing pages in CMS with email capture integration
Ensure proper metadata, internal linking, and conversion flow
Maintain consistency between page copy and downloadable asset


Who This Role Is For

This role is for someone who is highly proficient in AI-assisted content production and can maintain quality at volume.

Required Skills


Strong experience using AI writing tools effectively (knows the difference between edited output and raw AI content)
Ability to write clearly, directly, and without corporate or filler language
Experience publishing content in a CMS environment
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage workload independently
Ability to source relevant, context-aware visuals
Comfortable receiving editorial feedback and iterating quickly


Strong Plus


Experience with CMS platforms (e.g., Webflow or similar)
Email marketing experience
Basic SEO understanding (metadata, structure, internal linking)
Experience writing for B2B audiences (sales, revenue, or SaaS context)
Familiarity with CRM ecosystems
Experience with LinkedIn content creation and distribution


Schedule: Monday to Friday, 9 AM – 6 PM ET

Compensation: USD salary

Location: 100% remote opportunity

If this opportunity sounds good to you, send us your resume!

title: Head Of Strategic Expansion & Infrastructure Evaluation
skills: Strategic and analytical capabilities to assess new markets, evaluate infrastructure options, and build robust business cases for expansion.
Experience in project evaluation, including financial modeling, risk assessment, and scenario analysis for large-scale energy or infrastructure projects.
Background in infrastructure, energy, sustainability, or related fields, with familiarity in renewable energy, CCUS, or broader decarbonization initiatives.
Stakeholder management and negotiation skills to collaborate with public agencies, private partners, communities, and internal cross-functional teams.
Strong communication skills, including the ability to present complex findings in a clear, concise manner to senior leadership and non-technical audiences.
Proven leadership experience in building and guiding teams, setting priorities, and managing multiple concurrent initiatives in a fast-paced environment.
Bachelor’s degree in engineering, economics, business, public policy, or a related discipline; a relevant graduate degree (e.g., MBA, MS, MPP) is preferred.
Professional experience working in or with Latin American markets, ideally including Mexico, and comfort operating in a multicultural, multilingual context.
Commitment to climate action, environmental justice, and equitable infrastructure development, with a track record of integrating these principles into decision-making.
description:
About the job

Company Description Terracause is a climate-focused organization committed to accelerating the transition to sustainable energy and infrastructure solutions. Building on expertise in renewable energy and carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS), the company develops projects that support a decarbonized future and more resilient communities. Terracause collaborates with industry leaders, research institutions, and value chain partners to deliver high-impact, scalable solutions that reduce emissions and advance environmental justice. The organization is driven by a mission to set new standards in sustainability while creating long-term value for stakeholders and the planet.

Role Description The Head of Strategic Expansion & Infrastructure Evaluation is a full-time, on-site role based in Mexico City. This role is responsible for identifying, assessing, and prioritizing new markets and infrastructure opportunities that align with Terracause’s decarbonization and sustainability strategy. Day-to-day responsibilities include leading market and regulatory analyses, evaluating technical and economic feasibility of new infrastructure projects, and developing data-driven business cases for expansion. The role oversees due diligence activities, coordinates cross-functional input from technical, legal, financial, and policy teams, and prepares executive-ready recommendations for investment and partnership decisions. The Head of Strategic Expansion & Infrastructure Evaluation will also build and manage relationships with external stakeholders, including local authorities, infrastructure operators, community organizations, and potential partners, while establishing processes and metrics to track performance and risks across the expansion portfolio.

Qualifications

 

Strategic and analytical capabilities to assess new markets, evaluate infrastructure options, and build robust business cases for expansion.
Experience in project evaluation, including financial modeling, risk assessment, and scenario analysis for large-scale energy or infrastructure projects.
Background in infrastructure, energy, sustainability, or related fields, with familiarity in renewable energy, CCUS, or broader decarbonization initiatives.
Stakeholder management and negotiation skills to collaborate with public agencies, private partners, communities, and internal cross-functional teams.
Strong communication skills, including the ability to present complex findings in a clear, concise manner to senior leadership and non-technical audiences.
Proven leadership experience in building and guiding teams, setting priorities, and managing multiple concurrent initiatives in a fast-paced environment.
Bachelor’s degree in engineering, economics, business, public policy, or a related discipline; a relevant graduate degree (e.g., MBA, MS, MPP) is preferred.
Professional experience working in or with Latin American markets, ideally including Mexico, and comfort operating in a multicultural, multilingual context.
Commitment to climate action, environmental justice, and equitable infrastructure development, with a track record of integrating these principles into decision-making.

title: SEO Specialist
skills: Drive organic growth across multiple client websites through on-page SEO, technical optimization, keyword strategy, and site structure improvements.
Conduct in-depth keyword research, competitor analysis, and SEO audits to identify growth opportunities and technical issues.
Analyze performance data using tools such as Google Analytics, Google Search Console, Ahrefs, SEMrush, Screaming Frog, and Google Business Profile to generate actionable insights.
Develop and implement internal linking strategies, resolve keyword cannibalization issues, and optimize content architecture.
Design and execute Local SEO strategies, including Google Maps optimization and visibility improvements across local and AI-driven search surfaces.
Collaborate with writers, designers, developers, and SEO peers to test new ideas, improve deliverables, and optimize campaign performance.
Work directly with clients to understand business goals, communicate SEO insights clearly, and translate requirements into effective strategies.
Continuously test new SEO approaches, evaluate algorithm updates, and contribute insights that improve overall team performance.
Stay up to date with industry trends, attend webinars or conferences, and proactively bring new learnings into client strategies.
3+ years of hands-on SEO experience with proven results in improving rankings and organic traffic.
At least 2 years of experience in a client-facing SEO role within an agency environment.
Strong proficiency with SEO and analytics tools including Google Analytics, Google Search Console, and Google Business Profile.
Solid working knowledge of tools such as Ahrefs, SEMrush, Screaming Frog, or similar platforms.
Proven ability to design and execute both short-term and long-term SEO strategies that balance quick wins and sustainable growth.
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data, identify patterns, and adapt strategies under algorithm changes.
Ability to manage multiple client accounts independently in a fast-paced, fully remote environment.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to simplify complex SEO concepts for clients and stakeholders.
Experience with WordPress or other CMS platforms and basic understanding of HTML/CSS is a plus.
Exposure to digital PR, link building, or off-site SEO strategies is a strong advantage.
Experience in automotive SEO, local SEO, or multi-location businesses is considered a plus.
Bonus: Experience building and growing your own website with measurable SEO results.
Competitive salary paid in USD on a consistent monthly basis.
Fully remote role with flexibility to work from anywhere in Latin America or Europe.
10 paid vacation days annually plus U.S. federal holidays.
High level of ownership over SEO strategy and execution with freedom to test and innovate.
Collaborative, high-performance agency culture focused on experimentation and continuous improvement.
Access to SEO conferences, webinars, and ongoing professional development opportunities.
Supportive environment with strong mentorship and clear growth pathways.
Opportunity to work on impactful, real-world SEO projects in a specialized automotive niche.
Culture that values curiosity, data-driven decisions, and measurable results.
description:
About the job

This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a SEO Specialist based in Mexico.

As an SEO Specialist, you will take full ownership of organic search performance for a portfolio of high-growth automotive clients in a fully remote, performance-driven environment. This role goes beyond execution — it is designed for someone who thinks strategically, experiments continuously, and uses data to drive real ranking improvements. You will work closely with cross-functional teams including content creators, designers, and developers to deliver measurable SEO impact across multiple websites. Your work will directly influence organic traffic growth, local search visibility, and long-term digital performance. The environment encourages curiosity, testing, and evidence-based decision-making, where ideas are valued based on results rather than assumptions. This is an opportunity to shape SEO strategy end-to-end while working in a collaborative, fast-moving agency setting focused on real outcomes.

Accountabilities


Drive organic growth across multiple client websites through on-page SEO, technical optimization, keyword strategy, and site structure improvements.
Conduct in-depth keyword research, competitor analysis, and SEO audits to identify growth opportunities and technical issues.
Analyze performance data using tools such as Google Analytics, Google Search Console, Ahrefs, SEMrush, Screaming Frog, and Google Business Profile to generate actionable insights.
Develop and implement internal linking strategies, resolve keyword cannibalization issues, and optimize content architecture.
Design and execute Local SEO strategies, including Google Maps optimization and visibility improvements across local and AI-driven search surfaces.
Collaborate with writers, designers, developers, and SEO peers to test new ideas, improve deliverables, and optimize campaign performance.
Work directly with clients to understand business goals, communicate SEO insights clearly, and translate requirements into effective strategies.
Continuously test new SEO approaches, evaluate algorithm updates, and contribute insights that improve overall team performance.
Stay up to date with industry trends, attend webinars or conferences, and proactively bring new learnings into client strategies.


Requirements


3+ years of hands-on SEO experience with proven results in improving rankings and organic traffic.
At least 2 years of experience in a client-facing SEO role within an agency environment.
Strong proficiency with SEO and analytics tools including Google Analytics, Google Search Console, and Google Business Profile.
Solid working knowledge of tools such as Ahrefs, SEMrush, Screaming Frog, or similar platforms.
Proven ability to design and execute both short-term and long-term SEO strategies that balance quick wins and sustainable growth.
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data, identify patterns, and adapt strategies under algorithm changes.
Ability to manage multiple client accounts independently in a fast-paced, fully remote environment.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to simplify complex SEO concepts for clients and stakeholders.
Experience with WordPress or other CMS platforms and basic understanding of HTML/CSS is a plus.
Exposure to digital PR, link building, or off-site SEO strategies is a strong advantage.
Experience in automotive SEO, local SEO, or multi-location businesses is considered a plus.
Bonus: Experience building and growing your own website with measurable SEO results.


Benefits


Competitive salary paid in USD on a consistent monthly basis.
Fully remote role with flexibility to work from anywhere in Latin America or Europe.
10 paid vacation days annually plus U.S. federal holidays.
High level of ownership over SEO strategy and execution with freedom to test and innovate.
Collaborative, high-performance agency culture focused on experimentation and continuous improvement.
Access to SEO conferences, webinars, and ongoing professional development opportunities.
Supportive environment with strong mentorship and clear growth pathways.
Opportunity to work on impactful, real-world SEO projects in a specialized automotive niche.
Culture that values curiosity, data-driven decisions, and measurable results.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

 Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: SDR mexico
description:
About the job

Descripción del puesto:




CUSTOMERTOP es una empresa fundada en 2004 con una dilatada experiencia en la prospección comercial en empresas de todos los tamaños y de los más diversos sectores, en Europa y Latinoamérica.

Buscamos colaborador/a freelance para desarrollar tareas de concertación de visitas comerciales en el mercado colombiano, en modalidad TELETRABAJO, y que cuente con experiencia en ventas B2B y en trabajo en remoto, con alta responsabilidad y compromiso.




REQUISITOS:




-Experiencia de 2 años mínimo en ventas B2B de intangibles como software, seguros, marketing, etc

-Uso activo de linkedin

-Habilidades de investigación, prospección y seguimiento de leads.

-Oferta valida en todo MEXICO

-Excelente comunicación y persuasión

-Tener computador apto para teletrabajar (8 DE RAM Y WINDOWS) se realiza prueba




CONDICIONES:




- Home Office 100%

- Contrato freelance estable a 1 año renovable

- 8 horas diarias/40 horas semanales (Lunes a viernes) horario de oficina porque es la jornada de mayor contactabilidad

-Se descansan los feriados mexicanos oficiales

- Compensación económica 650 euros/mes fijos (No es negociable)




FUNCIONES:




-Emisión de llamadas en frio y envío de emails para agendar citas comerciales

-Manejo de redes sociales para contactar empresas mexicanas

- Gestión de Base de Datos en frio

-Prospección de clientes por redes sociales y manejo de bases

-Perfilar y contactar tomadores de decisiones.

¡Te estamos esperando para ser parte de nuestro equipo!




Si estas interesado envíame hoja de vida actualizada al correo alejandra.moreno@customertop.es

title: Desarrollador de Chatbot
description:
About the job

Allianz Partners

Is a world leader in B2B2C insurance and assistance, offering global solutions that span international health and life, travel insurance, automotive and assistance. Customer driven, our innovative experts are redefining insurance services by delivering future-ready, high-tech high-touch products and solutions that go beyond traditional insurance.

Our products are embedded seamlessly into our partners’ businesses or sold directly to customers and are available through four commercial brands: Allianz Assistance, Allianz Automotive, Allianz Travel and Allianz Care. Present in 75 countries, our 21,100 employees speak 70 languages, handle over 71 million cases each year, and are motivated to go the extra mile to offer peace of mind to our customers around the world.

We're looking for our new members!




This role is acting as global

AzP Conversational AI (CAI) Solution Engineer, located in the GCC Mexico.

Its mission is to help to develop and deploy effective Chatbots; Voicebots that meet the specific local needs.

This involves working closely with various internal and external stakeholders to understand the requirements, designing and building conversational AI systems that integrate with their existing technologies and core systems (e.g. CRM) and testing and optimizing the solution to ensure they deliver a high-quality user experience.




In order to be successful in this role, one will need:




Required Experience:




Knowledge of Chatbots, Conversational AI frameworks and technologies, such as STT (Speech-To-Text), TTS (Text-To-Speech), SSML (Speech Synthesis Markup Language) and NLP (Natural Language Processing), LLMs (Large Language Models).




Experience with Java Script, APIs, and other relevant technologies.




Understanding of the

principles of Conversational design and user experience (UX) for Conversational interfaces.




Expertise in programming languages such as JavaScript, Python, or similar

programming languages.




Understanding of

understanding of REST APIs and web services.




Knowledge of Agile methodology and experience in working with project management tools.




Experience in large-scale enterprise infrastructure and application landscape.




Advanced English level and Computer Science/Systems Engineering degree

is required.




Conversational AI Platform certification (Infobip, Cognigy etc.) is a plus.




Key Responsibilities:







Designing and developing chatbots, conversational flows, user interfaces and integration points/ extensions for Chat/Voicebots.




Developing and documenting processes and procedures for building and deploying Conversational AI solution.




Support Conversational AI operations projects with expertise.




Documenting technical changes, workflow related tasks and provide development status update where applicable.




Collaborating with other engineers and stakeholders to ensure that solutions are designed to be scalable, reliable, and maintainable.




Troubleshoot and resolve issues.




Continuously and self-independently drives asset enhancements and concept creation.




Providing technical guidance and support to AzP stakeholders during the development and deployment of their Conversational AI use cases.




Offer:




Direct hiring.




Remote work (must be based in Mexico City).




Full time

Monday - Friday schedule.




35-40k MXN Monthly salary (according to experience) + Food Coupons

+ Law benefits + Annual bonus + Health Insurance + Major Medical Expenses Insurance + Saving Fund.







Allianz Group is one of the most trusted insurance and asset management companies in the world. Caring for our employees, their ambitions, dreams and challenges, is what makes us a unique employer. Together we can build an environment where everyone feels empowered and has the confidence to explore, to grow and to shape a better future for our customers and the world around us.




We at Allianz believe in a diverse and inclusive workforce and are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We encourage you to bring your whole self to work, no matter where you are from, what you look like, who you love or what you believe in. We therefore welcome applications regardless of ethnicity or cultural background, age, gender, nationality, religion, disability or sexual orientation. Great to have you on board. Let's care for tomorrow.

title: Associate Retail Marketing Manager (Bilingual - English/Spanish)
skills: Job Title: Associate Retail Marketing Manager - Central & South America

Job Summary: Supports the Global Sales organization & Retail Marketing team to pitch and manage execution of retail activations in Central & South America. This individual will partner closely with internal stakeholders and external partners to create impactful consumer experiences where Pokémon products are sold.
FLSA Classification (US Only): Exempt
People Manager: No
Develop proposals with Central & South America TCG Sales team to secure incremental product placements and promotional opportunities at priority retail accounts.
Partner with Bellevue-based marketing teams, creative & brand services, legal, finance, and other internal departments to ensure in-market experiences adhere to TPCi global standards.
Identify new opportunities in the retail landscape for Pokémon that connect brand equity with local cultures.
Manage project execution, communicate status, and analyze performance with a focus on continuous improvement.
Three (3) to four (4) years of relevant professional experience or a demonstrated equivalent level of expertise.
Bachelor's degree in a related field or a demonstrated equivalent level of applicable experience.
Languages: Excellent verbal and communication skills in both English and Latin American Spanish, at near-native level.
Passion to create unique programs that combine Pokémon and partner goals
Experience working with external partners and able to communicate clearly and troubleshoot challenges diplomatically
Demonstrable ability to project manage, work cross functionally, and oversee multiple projects with various stakeholders
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office.

Passion for Pokémon: Develops an understanding of the Pokémon brand, the impact it has on our people, culture, business, fans, and communities, and applying that knowledge and passion to everything you do.

Challenging the Expected: Approaches challenges with curiosity and creativity, embracing the possibility of failure as an opportunity to learn something new, develop innovative ideas, solve complex problems and identify unique opportunities.

Integrity and Respect: Demonstrates integrity and respect by leading with empathy, listening to others, seeking out different perspectives, and taking personal responsibility for decisions, actions, and results.

Dedicated to Quality: Takes ownership to maintain and promote high standards, looks for new ways to learn and improve, and embraces a growth mindset to seek and apply feedback from others in an effort to continuously improve.
Building Relationships: Develops and strengthens relationships, adopting a “team first” mentality and working collaboratively to solve problems and meet shared goals.

Delighting Customers: Listens and understands the interests and needs of our customers and stakeholders, making them feel heard and important, and embracing these learnings to continue delivering a unique Pokémon experience.
An innovative culture driven by impact, delivering meaningful outcomes.
Company events that celebrate the spirit of Pokémon.
Competitive cash-based compensation programs.
100% employer-paid healthcare premiums for you.
Generous paid family leave.
Employer-paid life insurance.
Employer-paid long and short-term income protection insurance.
US Employees: 401k Employer Matching.
UK/IRE/MX Employees: Pension Employer Contributions.
Fitness reimbursement.
Commuter benefit.
LinkedIn learning.
Comprehensive relocation package for certain roles.
Hybrid work environment.
description:
About the job

Get To Know The Pokémon Company International

The Pokémon Company International manages the Pokémon property outside of Asia and is responsible for brand management, licensing and marketing, the Pokémon Trading Card Game, the animated TV series, home entertainment, and the official Pokémon website. Pokémon was launched in Japan in 1996 and today is one of the most popular children’s entertainment properties in the world.

Learn more online at corporate.pokemon.com and pokemon.com.

 

Note: Please include an English version of your resume with your application

Get to know the role


Job Title: Associate Retail Marketing Manager - Central & South America 
Job Summary: Supports the Global Sales organization & Retail Marketing team to pitch and manage execution of retail activations in Central & South America. This individual will partner closely with internal stakeholders and external partners to create impactful consumer experiences where Pokémon products are sold. 
FLSA Classification (US Only): Exempt 
People Manager: No 


What you’ll do


Develop proposals with Central & South America TCG Sales team to secure incremental product placements and promotional opportunities at priority retail accounts. 
Partner with Bellevue-based marketing teams, creative & brand services, legal, finance, and other internal departments to ensure in-market experiences adhere to TPCi global standards. 
Identify new opportunities in the retail landscape for Pokémon that connect brand equity with local cultures. 
Manage project execution, communicate status, and analyze performance with a focus on continuous improvement. 


What you’ll bring


Three (3) to four (4) years of relevant professional experience or a demonstrated equivalent level of expertise. 
Bachelor's degree in a related field or a demonstrated equivalent level of applicable experience. 
Languages: Excellent verbal and communication skills in both English and Latin American Spanish, at near-native level.
Passion to create unique programs that combine Pokémon and partner goals 
Experience working with external partners and able to communicate clearly and troubleshoot challenges diplomatically 
Demonstrable ability to project manage, work cross functionally, and oversee multiple projects with various stakeholders 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 
Proficiency in Microsoft Office. 


How You’ll Be Successful


Passion for Pokémon: Develops an understanding of the Pokémon brand, the impact it has on our people, culture, business, fans, and communities, and applying that knowledge and passion to everything you do.
Challenging the Expected: Approaches challenges with curiosity and creativity, embracing the possibility of failure as an opportunity to learn something new, develop innovative ideas, solve complex problems and identify unique opportunities. 
Integrity and Respect: Demonstrates integrity and respect by leading with empathy, listening to others, seeking out different perspectives, and taking personal responsibility for decisions, actions, and results.
Dedicated to Quality: Takes ownership to maintain and promote high standards, looks for new ways to learn and improve, and embraces a growth mindset to seek and apply feedback from others in an effort to continuously improve. 
Building Relationships: Develops and strengthens relationships, adopting a “team first” mentality and working collaboratively to solve problems and meet shared goals. 
Delighting Customers: Listens and understands the interests and needs of our customers and stakeholders, making them feel heard and important, and embracing these learnings to continue delivering a unique Pokémon experience.


What To Expect


An innovative culture driven by impact, delivering meaningful outcomes.
Company events that celebrate the spirit of Pokémon.
Competitive cash-based compensation programs.
100% employer-paid healthcare premiums for you.
Generous paid family leave.
Employer-paid life insurance.
Employer-paid long and short-term income protection insurance.
US Employees: 401k Employer Matching.
UK/IRE/MX Employees: Pension Employer Contributions.
Fitness reimbursement.
Commuter benefit.
LinkedIn learning.
Comprehensive relocation package for certain roles.
Hybrid work environment.


The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by people assigned to this role. They are not to be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and skills required. Employees may be required to perform duties outside of their normal responsibilities from time to time, as needed. For roles in the United Kingdom, candidates will need the right to work. In some cases, and for some roles, the Company may be able to arrange a visa. For roles in Ireland, this role requires candidates to have the right to live and work in the Republic of Ireland. However, we welcome applications from all nationalities and may consider supporting an employment permit application, in appropriate and suitable cases.

The Pokémon Company International is committed to the inclusion of all qualified applicants for consideration in our job application process. If you require reasonable accommodation to complete a job application, pre-employment testing, or a job interview, or to otherwise participate in the hiring process, please contact the Talent Acquisition team at accommodationrequest_ta@pokemon.com.

title: Analista de Comunicación
description:
About the job

Descripción de la empresa

Bosch es una empresa global líder en tecnología (Innovación para tu vida) para los más diversos sectores de negocios. En México, cuenta con alrededor de 18 mil empleados y 16 ubicaciones a lo largo del territorio nacional. Dada su escala, se trata de una empresa multitemática, diversa y multicultural.

Analista de Comunicación con foco en el desarrollo de contenido multimedia, análisis de contenido, diseño de campañas y relacionamiento para públicos externos.

Licenciatura en Comunicación, Periodismo, Letras o afnes.

description:
About the job

En undertk studio estamos buscando a una persona técnica para sumarse a nuestro tech studio.

Buscamos a alguien con experiencia real trabajando con Shopify, Webflow y herramientas de AI aplicadas al trabajo técnico. Es un perfil específico, así que queremos explicarlo con claridad para que haga sentido de ambos lados.




Este rol es para una persona que disfrute construir, resolver y mejorar. Alguien que pueda tomar una necesidad técnica, entender el problema, proponer una solución práctica y ejecutarla con buen criterio.

El día a día puede incluir desarrollo y ajustes en sitios de Shopify y Webflow, implementación de mejoras, conexión de herramientas, automatizaciones, solución de bugs, optimización de procesos y uso de AI para trabajar de forma más eficiente.




No buscamos únicamente a alguien que “conozca” las plataformas. Buscamos a alguien que se sienta cómodo resolviendo problemas reales, aprendiendo rápido y usando tecnología con criterio.

Diseño visual es un plus, pero no es indispensable. Lo más importante es la parte técnica, la capacidad de ejecución, la curiosidad y el criterio para encontrar soluciones.




Sobre undertk studio

Somos una agencia moderna, orientada a objetivos y con una forma de trabajo práctica. Trabajamos con marcas de distintas industrias en proyectos de marketing, creatividad, performance, tecnología y crecimiento.




Nos gusta movernos rápido, trabajar con orden y cuidar el balance entre vida y trabajo. Creemos en equipos responsables, autónomos y con espacio para proponer.




Responsabilidades principales

Construir, ajustar y mantener sitios en Shopify y Webflow.
Implementar mejoras técnicas en sitios existentes.
Resolver bugs, errores de configuración y problemas técnicos.
Conectar herramientas, formularios, CRMs, automatizaciones y flujos de trabajo.
Usar herramientas de AI para acelerar procesos técnicos, prototipar, documentar y resolver problemas.
Detectar oportunidades de mejora en sitios, procesos y flujos digitales.
Colaborar con equipos creativos, de estrategia, contenido y performance.
Proponer soluciones prácticas, claras y accionables.




Requisitos indispensables

Experiencia real trabajando con Shopify.
Experiencia real trabajando con Webflow.
Uso avanzado de herramientas de AI aplicadas a desarrollo, automatización o procesos técnicos.
Capacidad para resolver problemas técnicos de forma autónoma.
Orden, responsabilidad y buena comunicación.
Criterio para priorizar, proponer y ejecutar.
Ganas de aprender y mejorar constantemente.




Sería un plus

Conocimiento de diseño web o UI.
Experiencia con herramientas no-code o low-code.
Experiencia conectando APIs, formularios, CRMs o plataformas de automatización.
Conocimiento básico de SEO técnico.

Modalidad y compensación

Buen balance entre vida y trabajo.
Ambiente profesional, cercano y orientado a objetivos.

¿Qué has construido o trabajado recientemente en Webflow?
¿Qué herramientas de AI usas en tu flujo técnico y para qué?

Este rol puede ser ideal para alguien que combina desarrollo web, mentalidad medium/low-code, automatización y uso avanzado de AI para resolver problemas técnicos.




Si tienes este perfil, nos encantará conocerte.

skills: Job Title: Associate Retail Marketing Manager - Central & South America

Job Summary: Supports the Global Sales organization & Retail Marketing team to pitch and manage execution of retail activations in Central & South America. This individual will partner closely with internal stakeholders and external partners to create impactful consumer experiences where Pokémon products are sold.
FLSA Classification (US Only): Exempt
People Manager: No
Develop proposals with Central & South America TCG Sales team to secure incremental product placements and promotional opportunities at priority retail accounts.
Partner with Bellevue-based marketing teams, creative & brand services, legal, finance, and other internal departments to ensure in-market experiences adhere to TPCi global standards.
Identify new opportunities in the retail landscape for Pokémon that connect brand equity with local cultures.
Manage project execution, communicate status, and analyze performance with a focus on continuous improvement.
Three (3) to four (4) years of relevant professional experience or a demonstrated equivalent level of expertise.
Bachelor's degree in a related field or a demonstrated equivalent level of applicable experience.
Languages: Excellent verbal and communication skills in both English and Latin American Spanish, at near-native level.
Passion to create unique programs that combine Pokémon and partner goals
Experience working with external partners and able to communicate clearly and troubleshoot challenges diplomatically
Demonstrable ability to project manage, work cross functionally, and oversee multiple projects with various stakeholders
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office.

Passion for Pokémon: Develops an understanding of the Pokémon brand, the impact it has on our people, culture, business, fans, and communities, and applying that knowledge and passion to everything you do.

Challenging the Expected: Approaches challenges with curiosity and creativity, embracing the possibility of failure as an opportunity to learn something new, develop innovative ideas, solve complex problems and identify unique opportunities.

Integrity and Respect: Demonstrates integrity and respect by leading with empathy, listening to others, seeking out different perspectives, and taking personal responsibility for decisions, actions, and results.

Dedicated to Quality: Takes ownership to maintain and promote high standards, looks for new ways to learn and improve, and embraces a growth mindset to seek and apply feedback from others in an effort to continuously improve.
Building Relationships: Develops and strengthens relationships, adopting a “team first” mentality and working collaboratively to solve problems and meet shared goals.

Delighting Customers: Listens and understands the interests and needs of our customers and stakeholders, making them feel heard and important, and embracing these learnings to continue delivering a unique Pokémon experience.
An innovative culture driven by impact, delivering meaningful outcomes.
Company events that celebrate the spirit of Pokémon.
Competitive cash-based compensation programs.
100% employer-paid healthcare premiums for you.
Generous paid family leave.
Employer-paid life insurance.

UK/IRE/MX Employees: Pension Employer Contributions.

Commuter benefit.
LinkedIn learning.

Hybrid work environment.
description:
About the job

Get To Know The Pokémon Company International

The Pokémon Company International manages the Pokémon property outside of Asia and is responsible for brand management, licensing and marketing, the Pokémon Trading Card Game, the animated TV series, home entertainment, and the official Pokémon website. Pokémon was launched in Japan in 1996 and today is one of the most popular children’s entertainment properties in the world.

Learn more online at corporate.pokemon.com and pokemon.com.

 

Note: Please include an English version of your resume with your application

Get to know the role


Job Title: Associate Retail Marketing Manager - Central & South America

Three (3) to four (4) years of relevant professional experience or a demonstrated equivalent level of expertise. 
Bachelor's degree in a related field or a demonstrated equivalent level of applicable experience.

Passion to create unique programs that combine Pokémon and partner goals 
Experience working with external partners and able to communicate clearly and troubleshoot challenges diplomatically 
Demonstrable ability to project manage, work cross functionally, and oversee multiple projects with various stakeholders 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 
Proficiency in Microsoft Office. 


How You’ll Be Successful


Passion for Pokémon: Develops an understanding of the Pokémon brand, the impact it has on our people, culture, business, fans, and communities, and applying that knowledge and passion to everything you do.
Challenging the Expected: Approaches challenges with curiosity and creativity, embracing the possibility of failure as an opportunity to learn something new, develop innovative ideas, solve complex problems and identify unique opportunities. 
Integrity and Respect: Demonstrates integrity and respect by leading with empathy, listening to others, seeking out different perspectives, and taking personal responsibility for decisions, actions, and results.
Dedicated to Quality: Takes ownership to maintain and promote high standards, looks for new ways to learn and improve, and embraces a growth mindset to seek and apply feedback from others in an effort to continuously improve. 
Building Relationships: Develops and strengthens relationships, adopting a “team first” mentality and working collaboratively to solve problems and meet shared goals. 
Delighting Customers: Listens and understands the interests and needs of our customers and stakeholders, making them feel heard and important, and embracing these learnings to continue delivering a unique Pokémon experience.


What To Expect


An innovative culture driven by impact, delivering meaningful outcomes.
Company events that celebrate the spirit of Pokémon.
Competitive cash-based compensation programs.
100% employer-paid healthcare premiums for you.
Generous paid family leave.
Employer-paid life insurance.

The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by people assigned to this role. They are not to be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and skills required. Employees may be required to perform duties outside of their normal responsibilities from time to time, as needed. For roles in the United Kingdom, candidates will need the right to work. In some cases, and for some roles, the Company may be able to arrange a visa. For roles in Ireland, this role requires candidates to have the right to live and work in the Republic of Ireland. However, we welcome applications from all nationalities and may consider supporting an employment permit application, in appropriate and suitable cases.

The Pokémon Company International is committed to the inclusion of all qualified applicants for consideration in our job application process. If you require reasonable accommodation to complete a job application, pre-employment testing, or a job interview, or to otherwise participate in the hiring process, please contact the Talent Acquisition team at accommodationrequest_ta@pokemon.com.

description:

Our products are embedded seamlessly into our partners’ businesses or sold directly to customers and are available through four commercial brands: Allianz Assistance, Allianz Automotive, Allianz Travel and Allianz Care.
